tasks.title: Establish Foundation Types and Environment Logic
tasks.description: Define the core data models, environment enums, and the target derivation logic in environment.ts.
tasks.details: Create 'src/environment.ts' to export 'SkillsEnvironment' ('windows' | 'wsl' | 'both') and 'RuntimeTargetName'. Implement 'deriveRuntimeTargets' to map user selection to concrete targets. Update 'src/types.ts' to include environment metadata in 'SkillInfo' and 'PersistedSettings'. Add 'RuntimeAdapter' interface in 'src/runtime/types.ts' covering path expansion, exists, stat, readFile, readDir, and exec.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for 'deriveRuntimeTargets' with various host OS and config combinations. Compile-time check for interface adherence in mock adapters.

subtasks.title: Define Environment Types and Enums
subtasks.description: Create core environment type definitions and enums in the environment module.
subtasks.details: Create 'src/environment.ts' and export 'SkillsEnvironment' as a union of 'windows' | 'wsl' | 'both'. Define 'RuntimeTargetName' to represent specific execution contexts. These types will serve as the foundation for multi-environment support.
subtasks.testStrategy: Compile-time validation of TypeScript types and literals to ensure correct mapping of allowed environment strings.

subtasks.title: Implement Runtime Target Derivation Logic
subtasks.description: Develop the logic to determine active runtime targets based on host OS and user settings.
subtasks.details: Implement the 'deriveRuntimeTargets' function in 'src/environment.ts'. This function should take the current platform and user selection as input and return an array of concrete 'RuntimeTargetName' values (e.g., if selection is 'both', it returns both Windows and WSL targets).
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for 'deriveRuntimeTargets' covering scenarios for Windows host, WSL availability, and 'both' environment configuration.

subtasks.title: Define RuntimeAdapter Interface and Update Core Types
subtasks.description: Establish the standard interface for runtime operations and update SkillInfo/PersistedSettings models.
subtasks.details: Create 'src/runtime/types.ts' to define the 'RuntimeAdapter' interface with methods: expandPath, exists, stat, readFile, readDir, and exec. Update 'src/types.ts' to include environment metadata in 'SkillInfo' and 'PersistedSettings' interfaces to support multi-target persistence.
subtasks.testStrategy: Compile-time check for interface adherence in mock adapters and validation of updated JSON schemas for settings migration.

tasks.title: Implement Windows Runtime Adapter
tasks.description: Create a runtime adapter for native Windows operations using Node.js fs and shell detection.
tasks.details: Implement 'src/runtime/windowsRuntime.ts' using native 'fs/promises' and 'os.homedir()'. The adapter must expand '~' to the Windows user profile. Command execution should utilize the existing shell detection logic (PowerShell/cmd) from 'executor.ts' but encapsulated within the adapter. Ensure paths are normalized using 'path.win32'.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests with 'mock-fs' to verify path expansion and file reads. Mock 'child_process' to verify command execution using Windows-specific shells.

subtasks.title: Implement Path Normalization and Home Expansion
subtasks.description: Develop the utility logic within the Windows adapter to expand the '~' character to the Windows user profile path and normalize paths using Windows conventions.
subtasks.details: Use 'os.homedir()' to retrieve the base path for expansion. Implement a 'normalizePath' method that utilizes 'path.win32.join' and 'path.win32.normalize' to ensure all separators are consistent Windows backslashes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests verifying that '~' expands to the correct 'C:\Users\...' path and that forward slashes are converted to backslashes.

subtasks.title: Implement Filesystem Operations for Windows Adapter
subtasks.description: Create the implementation for the RuntimeAdapter filesystem methods including stat, exists, readFile, and readDir using native Node.js fs/promises.
subtasks.details: Wrap 'fs.promises' functions. Every path input must first pass through the path normalization and expansion logic from Subtask 1 to ensure compatibility with Windows-specific file paths.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests using 'mock-fs' to simulate Windows directory structures and verify that 'readFile' and 'stat' return correct metadata and content.

subtasks.title: Integrate Shell Detection and Command Execution
subtasks.description: Implement the 'exec' method in the adapter by encapsulating existing logic to detect whether PowerShell or CMD is the active Windows shell.
subtasks.details: Port shell detection logic from 'executor.ts'. The 'exec' method should use 'child_process' to run commands. Ensure the execution environment sets the current working directory to the expanded Windows path provided.
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock 'child_process' to verify that commands are prefixed with the correct shell executable (powershell.exe or cmd.exe) based on environment detection.

tasks.title: Implement WSL Runtime Adapter
tasks.description: Create a runtime adapter that executes filesystem and shell operations inside WSL via wsl.exe.
tasks.details: Implement 'src/runtime/wslRuntime.ts'. Key features: 1. Get WSL home using 'wsl.exe -- bash -lc "echo $HOME"'. 2. Execute commands using 'wsl.exe -d <distro> -- <shell> -lc "<command>"'. 3. For file operations (read/exists/readdir), execute bash-native commands (cat, test -e, ls -p) through 'wsl.exe' instead of Windows 'fs'. Handle escaping for bash commands using standard POSIX shell quoting patterns.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration tests on a Windows host with WSL installed. Mock 'child_process' to verify that command strings correctly include '-d' and '--' flags.

subtasks.title: Implement WSL Environment Discovery and Home Directory Resolution
subtasks.description: Implement logic to identify the default WSL distribution and retrieve the user's home directory path using 'wsl.exe'.
subtasks.details: Create 'src/runtime/wslRuntime.ts'. Implement home directory retrieval using the command: 'wsl.exe -- bash -lc "echo $HOME"'. Ensure the result is cached for the lifecycle of the adapter.
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock child_process.exec to return a standard POSIX path and verify that the adapter correctly parses and stores the result.

subtasks.title: Create Bash Execution Wrapper with Secure Quoting
subtasks.description: Develop a robust execution wrapper that pipes commands through 'wsl.exe' using standard POSIX shell quoting patterns.
subtasks.details: Implement the 'exec' method using the format: 'wsl.exe -d <distro> -- <shell> -lc "<command>"'. Develop a internal helper function to escape single quotes and special characters for bash-compatible command strings.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests verifying that complex command strings (e.g., containing spaces, nested quotes, and dollar signs) are correctly transformed before being passed to wsl.exe.

subtasks.title: Implement Virtual Filesystem Operations via WSL CLI
subtasks.description: Map adapter filesystem calls to native bash commands executed through the wsl.exe shell wrapper.
subtasks.details: Map 'exists' to 'test -e', 'readFile' to 'cat', and 'readDir' to 'ls -p'. Ensure all Windows-style paths are converted to forward-slash POSIX paths before being sent to the bash commands.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests comparing the output of 'readDir' and 'readFile' against known files within a test WSL distribution to ensure parity with the native Windows adapter.

subtasks.title: Develop WSL Error Parsing and Response Normalization
subtasks.description: Handle wsl.exe exit codes and parse stderr to provide consistent error messages across the adapter.
subtasks.details: Implement logic to intercept 'wsl.exe' process errors. Normalize stderr output (e.g., mapping 'No such file' to standard ENOENT codes) and ensure line endings are handled (converting CRLF to LF where appropriate).
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests mocking non-zero exit codes from wsl.exe to verify the adapter correctly identifies and propagates errors like 'Distro not found' or 'Permission denied'.

tasks.title: Implement Environment-Aware Path Resolver
tasks.description: Create a path resolver that uses runtime adapters to expand and normalize skill paths for specific targets.
tasks.details: Implement 'src/pathResolver.ts'. It should handle multi-path strings (semicolon-separated) and resolve them independently for each active runtime target (Windows/WSL). In 'Both' mode, it must produce two distinct lists of 'ResolvedSkillRoot' objects, each carrying its environment label. Use 'formatDisplayPath' to prefix paths with 'Windows:' or 'WSL:'.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests verifying that '~/.agents/skills' resolves to C:\... for Windows and /home/... for WSL targets simultaneously in 'Both' mode.

subtasks.title: Implement Semicolon-Separated Path Parsing Logic
subtasks.description: Create a utility function within src/pathResolver.ts to split multi-path strings and normalize the individual segments.
subtasks.details: Implement logic to handle strings like 'path/one;path/two; path/three'. Use native string splitting and trimming. Ensure the logic is robust against empty segments, duplicate paths, or trailing semicolons.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests with various string inputs including single paths, multiple paths with irregular whitespace, and strings with redundant semicolons.

subtasks.title: Develop Multi-Target Resolution Loop using Adapters
subtasks.description: Develop the core resolution loop that iterates over active runtime targets (Windows/WSL) and utilizes runtime adapters for path expansion.
subtasks.details: Integrate with deriveRuntimeTargets to determine active targets. For each target, call the corresponding RuntimeAdapter's path expansion method for every path parsed in Subtask 1. Handle the 'Both' environment case by aggregating results from both Windows and WSL adapters.
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock the RuntimeAdapter interface and verify that the resolver invokes the correct expansion methods based on the SkillsEnvironment configuration.

subtasks.title: Implement ResolvedSkillRoot Creation and Display Path Formatting
subtasks.description: Finalize the resolver output by packaging results into ResolvedSkillRoot objects and applying environment-specific prefix formatting.
subtasks.details: Map resolution results into ResolvedSkillRoot objects containing environment labels and absolute paths. Implement the 'formatDisplayPath' utility to prefix paths with 'Windows:' or 'WSL:' for clarity in UI components. Ensure the final output is a flat list or grouped by target as required.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that the final output contains valid ResolvedSkillRoot objects and that 'formatDisplayPath' correctly identifies and prefixes the environment label to the absolute path.

tasks.title: Refactor Command Executor for Target Routing
tasks.description: Update executor.ts to route command execution to the appropriate runtime adapter based on environment and context.
tasks.details: Update 'src/executor.ts' to accept an optional 'target' or infer it from the 'cwd'. If environment is 'Both' and target is ambiguous, return a structured error. Use the 'RuntimeRegistry' (to be implemented in 'src/runtime/index.ts') to fetch the correct adapter. Ensure response metadata includes 'Execution Environment' and 'Shell' used.
tasks.testStrategy: Mock runtime adapters and verify that 'execCommandForTarget' calls the correct 'exec' method. Verify error handling for ambiguous 'Both' mode requests.

subtasks.title: Implement RuntimeRegistry for Adapter Management
subtasks.description: Create a central registry in 'src/runtime/index.ts' to manage and retrieve instances of environment-specific runtime adapters.
subtasks.details: Define a RuntimeRegistry class that holds instances of Windows and WSL adapters. Implement a lookup method that returns the correct adapter based on a target identifier string.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test the registry to ensure it returns the correct adapter instances for given keys and handles invalid keys with appropriate errors.

subtasks.title: Implement Target Inference Logic based on CWD
subtasks.description: Develop helper logic within the executor to determine the execution environment by inspecting the current working directory.
subtasks.details: Create logic to detect if a path is a WSL path (e.g., starting with \\wsl$) or a standard Windows path. This will serve as the fallback when no explicit target is provided to the executor.
subtasks.testStrategy: Provide various mock file paths (Windows, UNC, and WSL) to the inference function and verify it resolves the correct target environment for each.

subtasks.title: Refactor Execute Function and Metadata Reporting
subtasks.description: Update the main command execution function in 'src/executor.ts' to utilize the registry and include environment metadata in responses.
subtasks.details: Modify the 'execute' function to use RuntimeRegistry. Fetch the adapter based on target or inferred CWD. Add logic to return a structured error if the environment is set to 'Both' and the target is ambiguous. Include 'Execution Environment' and 'Shell' in the response object.
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock the runtime adapters and verify that the executor calls the correct 'exec' method. Ensure the resulting metadata matches the chosen environment.

tasks.title: Update Configuration and Settings Migration
tasks.description: Expose new environment settings in the UI and handle backward-compatible settings migration.
tasks.details: Update 'src/config.ts' with 'skillsEnvironment' (select), 'wslDistro' (text), 'windowsShellPath' (text), and 'wslShellPath' (text). Update 'src/settings.ts' to migrate legacy 'shellPath' to 'windowsShellPath'. Ensure 'default' sentinel resets to host-native defaults. Add help text as specified in the PRD.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for 'loadSettings' with legacy JSON files to ensure correct field migration. Verify schema validation for new config fields.

subtasks.title: Update Configuration Schema in config.ts
subtasks.description: Define and add the new environment-specific setting fields to the configuration schema in src/config.ts.
subtasks.details: Add 'skillsEnvironment' (select), 'wslDistro' (text), 'windowsShellPath' (text), and 'wslShellPath' (text) to 'src/config.ts'. Include validation rules and descriptive help text for each field as specified in the PRD.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that the configuration schema correctly validates inputs for new fields and handles invalid values.

subtasks.title: Implement Legacy Settings Migration and Sentinel Logic
subtasks.description: Develop logic in settings.ts to migrate legacy shellPath values and handle 'default' sentinels.
subtasks.details: Update 'src/settings.ts' to migrate legacy 'shellPath' keys to the new 'windowsShellPath' field. Implement logic so that the 'default' sentinel value resets configuration to host-native defaults during the migration process.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run unit tests for 'loadSettings' with legacy JSON configuration files to ensure correct field mapping and sentinel reset functionality.

tasks.title: Refactor Skill Scanner for Environment Isolation
tasks.description: Update scanner.ts to discover and list skills across multiple environments using runtime adapters.
tasks.details: Refactor 'scanSkills' in 'src/scanner.ts' to iterate through 'ResolvedSkillRoot' objects. Each root should use its associated 'RuntimeAdapter' for directory traversal and 'SKILL.md' detection. Maintain environment identity in the resulting 'SkillInfo' objects. Deduplicate skills by (environment, path).
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests using mock adapters to simulate a skill directory existing in both Windows and WSL. Verify scanner returns both with correct metadata.

subtasks.title: Update Scan Loop for Resolved Skill Roots
subtasks.description: Modify the core scanning loop in scanner.ts to iterate through an array of ResolvedSkillRoot objects.
subtasks.details: Change the 'scanSkills' function signature and logic to process multiple root objects. For each root, initialize the traversal context using its specific environment and path metadata provided by the configuration layer.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test with multiple mock ResolvedSkillRoot objects to ensure the loop executes once for each root and correctly aggregates results.

subtasks.title: Abstract Filesystem Operations using Runtime Adapters
subtasks.description: Replace direct Node.js 'fs' calls within the scanner with root-specific RuntimeAdapter methods.
subtasks.details: Refactor directory traversal and 'SKILL.md' detection to use adapter.readDir, adapter.exists, and adapter.readFile. This ensures that WSL skills are scanned via the WSL adapter while Windows skills use the native filesystem adapter.
subtasks.testStrategy: Inject mock adapters that return simulated directory structures. Verify that the scanner calls the correct adapter methods based on the root's environment.

subtasks.title: Integrate Environment Tagging and Skill Deduplication
subtasks.description: Update SkillInfo construction to include environment metadata and implement logic to prevent duplicate skill entries.
subtasks.details: Ensure each discovered skill is mapped to a 'SkillInfo' object containing its source environment. Implement a deduplication filter that uses a composite key of (environment, path) to handle overlaps between different runtime targets.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test verifying that scanning a directory present in both Windows and WSL environments results in two distinct SkillInfo entries with unique environment tags.

tasks.title: Update Skill File Operations
tasks.description: Refactor read_skill_file and list_skill_files to operate via the correct environment adapter.
tasks.details: Modify 'readSkillFile' and 'listSkillDirectory' in 'src/scanner.ts' to use the 'environment' property from 'SkillInfo' to select the runtime adapter. Ensure absolute path authorization checks are environment-specific (e.g., WSL paths authorized only against WSL roots).
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests verifying that attempting to read a WSL skill file via Windows 'fs' (or vice versa) is physically impossible with the new architecture.

subtasks.title: Refactor Skill File Operations to Use Environment Adapters
subtasks.description: Update 'readSkillFile' and 'listSkillDirectory' in 'src/scanner.ts' to retrieve and use the appropriate RuntimeAdapter based on the 'environment' property of the skill's metadata.
subtasks.details: Modify the logic to look up the adapter from the RuntimeRegistry using the skill's environment metadata. Replace direct Node.js 'fs' calls with adapter-specific methods like 'readFile' and 'readDir' to ensure operations occur in the correct runtime environment.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests to verify that 'readSkillFile' and 'listSkillDirectory' correctly route requests to the mocked Windows or WSL adapters based on the environment field in the SkillInfo.

subtasks.title: Implement Environment-Specific Path Authorization Checks
subtasks.description: Update the security logic in 'src/scanner.ts' to ensure that absolute path authorization is validated against the specific roots of the selected environment.
subtasks.details: Implement logic to ensure that WSL paths are only authorized against defined WSL roots and Windows paths against Windows roots. This prevents cross-environment path traversal and ensures that a skill assigned to one environment cannot access files in another.
subtasks.testStrategy: Security tests attempting to access a WSL root using a Windows-configured skill and vice versa, expecting authorization failures and blocked file access.

tasks.title: Integrate Environment Metadata into Tools and Prompts
tasks.description: Update toolsProvider.ts and preprocessor.ts to expose environment info to the model and user.
tasks.details: 1. Update 'list_skills', 'read_skill_file', and 'run_command' tools to return environment, shell, and displayPath in the response. 2. Update 'src/preprocessor.ts' to include the environment label (e.g., 'WSL:/home/...') in the injected prompt locations so the model can refer to them correctly.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify tool response JSON schema includes new metadata fields. Verify injected system prompt contains correct environment-prefixed paths.

subtasks.title: Update Tool Response Metadata and Schemas
subtasks.description: Modify the tool return values in toolsProvider.ts to include environment, shell, and display path metadata.
subtasks.details: Update the implementation of 'list_skills', 'read_skill_file', and 'run_command' in 'src/toolsProvider.ts' to enrich the return objects with environment info (windows/wsl), the shell used, and the formatted display path. Ensure the JSON schema definitions for these tools reflect the additional fields for model clarity.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that tool response JSON outputs contain the expected 'environment', 'shell', and 'displayPath' fields when executed via integration tests.

subtasks.title: Inject Environment Labels into Preprocessor Prompts
subtasks.description: Modify src/preprocessor.ts to include environment prefixes for path locations injected into the system prompt.
subtasks.details: Update the logic in 'src/preprocessor.ts' that builds the system context. For every skill root path injected, ensure it is prefixed with its environment label (e.g., 'WSL:' or 'Windows:') provided by the path resolver. This allows the model to distinguish between identical paths in different environments during multi-target modes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Examine the generated system prompt via debug logs to confirm that all file paths include the correct environment-specific prefixes.

tasks.title: Update Bootstrap Logic and Add Diagnostics
tasks.description: Ensure skill bootstrapping is environment-aware and add WSL availability probes.
tasks.details: Update 'src/setup.ts' to perform bootstrap operations (creating directories, copying samples) through the runtime adapters. Add a 'probeWsl' method to 'wslRuntime.ts' to check for 'wsl.exe' existence. If WSL is selected but unavailable, return actionable errors instead of falling back to Windows.
tasks.testStrategy: Simulate 'wsl.exe' missing and verify that the plugin reports a specific error when 'WSL' mode is active. Verify bootstrap creates files in WSL when selected.

subtasks.title: Refactor setup.ts for Environment-Aware Bootstrapping
subtasks.description: Update the core bootstrap logic in 'src/setup.ts' to utilize RuntimeAdapter methods for all filesystem operations.
subtasks.details: Identify all calls to Node.js 'fs' module within 'src/setup.ts'. Replace them with 'adapter.mkdir', 'adapter.exists', and 'adapter.writeFile' calls. Ensure the bootstrap process iterates through all targets returned by 'deriveRuntimeTargets' to ensure both Windows and WSL environments are initialized when applicable.
subtasks.testStrategy: Execute the bootstrap command and verify that the '.skills' directory and sample files are created correctly in both the local Windows path and the WSL distribution path.

subtasks.title: Implement WSL Availability Probing Logic
subtasks.description: Add a diagnostic method to 'src/runtime/wslRuntime.ts' to check for 'wsl.exe' existence and functionality.
subtasks.details: Implement a 'probeWsl' method in the WslRuntimeAdapter. Use 'exec' to run 'wsl.exe --status' or 'wsl.exe --list'. Handle ENOENT errors specifically to detect if the WSL binary is missing entirely from the system PATH.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests mocking the execution environment to return 'command not found' for 'wsl.exe' and verifying the probe returns a failure state.

subtasks.title: Implement User-Facing Diagnostic Error Handling
subtasks.description: Create actionable error messages for the UI when the selected environment fails the availability probe.
subtasks.details: Update the environment initialization sequence to call 'probeWsl' if the environment setting is 'wsl' or 'both'. If the probe fails, instead of silently falling back to Windows, throw a descriptive error recommending WSL installation or configuration changes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Manual verification by setting 'skillsEnvironment' to 'wsl' on a machine without WSL configured and confirming the application displays the specified actionable error message.
